question: EC2 instance unreachable. Network troubleshooting?
answer: `aws ec2 describe-network-interfaces`, `aws ec2 describe-security-groups`, `aws ec2 describe-network-acls`

question: Grant S3 bucket access to another account.
List objects in an S3 bucket.
Get the latest version of the AWS CLI.
answer: Use your system's package manager (e.g., `pip install --upgrade awscli`)  // Special case

question: Get help on an AWS CLI command.
Add an inbound rule to a security group.
Remove an inbound rule from a security group.
Change the default AWS region.
answer: Modify the `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION` environment variable or use the `--region` option with CLI commands.

question: List all available AWS services.
answer: No single CLI command; check the AWS documentation. // Special case

question: Get the ARN of a resource.
answer: Use the appropriate `describe` or `list` command for the resource type.

question: Check for updates to the AWS CLI.
answer: Check your system's package manager (e.g., `pip list --outdated awscli`)

question: Get details of an S3 object.
Generate a pre-signed URL for an S3 object.
Sync a local directory to S3.
List EC2 instances with a specific tag.
Remove a tag from an EC2 instance.
Get details of a CloudWatch metric.
Add a user to an IAM group.
Attach an IAM policy to a user.
Detach an IAM policy from a user.
List DynamoDB tables in a specific region.
List ECR repositories.
answer: `aws ecr describe-repositories`  // Lists available ECR repositories

question: Create an ECR repository.
answer: `aws ecr create-repository --repository-name <repository-name>` // Creates a new repository

question: Delete an ECR repository.
answer: `aws ecr delete-repository --repository-name <repository-name>`  // Deletes a repository (must be empty)

question: Push an image to ECR.
answer: `docker tag <local-image>:<tag> <account_id>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/<repository-name>:<tag>` followed by `docker push <account_id>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/<repository-name>:<tag>` // Pushes a local Docker image to ECR

question: Pull an image from ECR.
answer: `docker pull <account_id>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/<repository-name>:<tag>` // Pulls a Docker image from ECR

question: List images in an ECR repository.
answer: `aws ecr list-images --repository-name <repository-name>`  // Lists images within a specific repository

question: Describe an ECR repository.
answer: `aws ecr describe-repositories --repository-names <repository-name>` // Shows detailed information about a repository

question: Delete an image from ECR.
answer: `aws ecr batch-delete-image --repository-name <repository-name> --image-ids imageDigest=<image-digest>` // Deletes a specific image from a repository

question: Get ECR lifecycle policy.
answer: `aws ecr get-lifecycle-policy --repository-name <repository-name>` // Retrieves the lifecycle policy for a repository (for managing image expiration)

question: Can you check ECR and how many repos dont have secret scanning enabled
List repositories with tag t1 and value v1
Get detailed vulnerability information for a specific image.
Get cost and usage report for last month.
answer: `aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=<start-date>,End=<end-date>` // Replace <start-date> and <end-date> with YYYY-MM-DD

question: Get cost forecast for next month.
answer: `aws ce get-cost-forecast --time-period Start=<start-date>,End=<end-date> --metric <metric> --granularity <granularity>`  // <metric> can be BLENDED_COST

question: Get cost and usage data by tag.
answer: `aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=<start-date>,End=<end-date> --metrics <metrics> --group-by Tag=<tag-key>` // <metrics> are comma-separated (e.g. BlendedCost

question: Get cost and usage summary by service.
Get my current AWS cost and usage breakdown by service.
answer: `aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=<yesterday>,End=<today> --granularity DAILY --metrics BlendedCost --group-by DIMENSION=SERVICE`. Replace <yesterday> and <today> with YYYY-MM-DD, for example using the date command:  Start=`date -v -1d +%Y-%m-%d`,End=`date +%Y-%m-%d`. If you are using a different shell replace the backtick quoted date commands with similar commands in your shell language.

question: Estimate AWS costs.
column3: `aws ce get-cost-and-usage` (for past costs) "// Updated with get-cost-forecast
